title: IronCache Helm chart values
description: Schema for deploy/helm/ironcache/values.yaml. Validated by Helm on every template/install; must accept the shipped defaults unchanged. Type/enum/port checks are enforced; cross-field arithmetic and semantic rules (odd replicas, quorum-safe PDB, non-noeviction policy) are documentation-only.

nameOverride.description: Override the chart name in resource names (the standard Helm fullname helper reads this; not in the default values.yaml but honored by _helpers.tpl).

fullnameOverride.description: Override the fully-qualified release name (the StatefulSet/Service base name). Used to run two releases in one namespace; honored by _helpers.tpl.

tag.description: Image tag. Empty string defaults to the chart appVersion (recommended). Avoid 'latest' in prod. Registry tag has no leading 'v'.

imagePullSecrets.description: Free-form list of localObjectReference {name: ...} for pulling from a private registry.

replicas.description: Number of IronCache nodes. For a Raft cluster prefer ODD (3, 5, 7) so majority quorum is unambiguous (odd is not schema-enforceable). 1 = standalone.

enabled.description: Turn on Raft clustering. When false the chart deploys independent standalone nodes (only sensible with replicas=1).

minReplicasToWrite.description: min_replicas_to_write write-side durability guardrail. 0 = disabled. Keep 0 with this chart: the static topology has no replicas, so >=1 fails writes with -NOREPLICAS.

raftSnapshotThreshold.description: Raft-log compaction threshold (entries above the last snapshot).

persistence.description: Durable snapshot + Raft log under data_dir.

enabled.description: Enable the PVC for durable persistence and the durable Raft log. When false the pod uses an emptyDir (data lost on reschedule) -- strongly discouraged for cluster.enabled=true, since the Raft log must survive a restart.

size.description: PVC size as a Kubernetes quantity, e.g. 10Gi.

storageClassName.description: StorageClass name. Empty string leaves the cluster default; set an SSD class in prod.

saveIntervalSecs.description: Periodic background save interval. 0 DISABLES the save policy AND the SIGTERM save-on-exit (a graceful stop then exits without writing). Keep > 0 for durability across rolling restarts.

saveMinChanges.description: Minimum keyspace writes since the last save before a periodic save fires.

auth.description: Client AUTH (requirepass). Strongly recommended in production.

enabled.description: Enable client AUTH. When true you must supply auth.password OR auth.existingSecret.

password.description: Inline password (chart creates the Secret). Prefer existingSecret in prod so the secret is not in Helm release history.

existingSecret.description: Reference an existing Secret instead of creating one. The key must be 'requirepass'.

clusterSecret.description: Shared intra-cluster secret (cluster_secret) presented in the peer handshake on the bus + replication links.

value.description: The cluster secret. Empty auto-generates on first install and is preserved across helm upgrade via a Secret lookup. Under GitOps / helm template / --dry-run the lookup is blind and would regenerate every sync, so set an explicit value (or existingSecret) for GitOps/reproducible installs. Do NOT force this when cluster.enabled -- forcing it breaks the normal install path.

existingSecret.description: Reference an existing Secret holding the cluster secret.

enabled.description: Enable cluster TLS. When true you must supply (cert AND key) OR existingSecret; ca is NOT required (#660).

ca.description: Peer-verification CA PEM. Empty = the self-signed cluster cert is used as its own CA (the chart writes cluster.ca = cert), so ca is never mandatory (#660).

existingSecret.description: Reference an existing Secret with keys cluster.crt / cluster.key / cluster.ca.

tls.description: Put the public client RESP listener on TLS (tls=on). Separate from clusterTls.

enabled.description: Enable client TLS. When true you must supply (cert AND key) OR existingSecret.

existingSecret.description: Reference an existing Secret with the client TLS material.

clientPort.description: Client RESP port. The cluster-bus (port+10000) and replication (port+20000) ports derive from this and must fit u16, so the max is 45535.

type.description: Client Service type. ClusterIP is correct for in-cluster clients; a single LB is not cluster-aware with Raft MOVED routing.

interval.description: Scrape interval as a Prometheus duration, e.g. 30s.

networkPolicy.description: NetworkPolicy for the cache StatefulSet (distinct from console.networkPolicy). Needs a NetworkPolicy-aware CNI; inert (fails open) on flannel / k3s default.

allowDNS.description: Allow DNS egress. REQUIRED for peer discovery in cluster mode; disabling it with egress enforced silently prevents cluster formation.

clientIngressFrom.description: Free-form NetworkPolicyPeer allowlist for the client RESP port (empty = open to all).

metricsIngressFrom.description: Free-form NetworkPolicyPeer allowlist for the metrics/probe port. Leave empty unless you have verified the CNI still admits kubelet probes.

extraEgress.description: Free-form extra egress rules appended verbatim.

resources.description: Cache container resource requests/limits. Free-form quantity maps (arbitrary resource keys allowed).

requests.description: Resource requests. RAISE the memory request toward the memory limit so the node reserves the true footprint and the cache is not a first-to-evict candidate.

limits.description: Resource limits. A MEMORY limit is strongly recommended (maxmemory auto-derives ~70% from it for OOM safety); NOT required -- without it the cache runs unbounded (a loud boot log says so). No cpu limit by default (avoids CFS throttling).

extraConfig.description: Extra IronCache TOML appended verbatim (maxmemory / maxmemory_policy / timeout etc). REAL TOML keys, underscore spelling; the parse is STRICT (a hyphen spelling like maxmemory-policy fails boot). Leave maxmemory unset for OOM-safe auto-derivation. Content is opaque to this schema (not enforceable here); a noeviction policy defeats the OOM guard (runtime warns).

logLevel.description: Log level passed as --log-level. The valid values are error, warn (=warning), info, debug (=verbose), trace; the binary lowercases the input, so any case is accepted. A case-insensitive pattern (not a lowercase enum) so a valid mixed-case value like 'WARN' is not wrongly rejected, while a typo still fails.

podAntiAffinity.description: Spread pods across nodes so a single node failure cannot take down a quorum.

type.description: soft = preferred (best-effort spread); hard = required (one pod per node, needs >= replicas nodes).

topologyKey.description: Node topology key for the anti-affinity spread.

nodeSelector.description: Free-form node selector (string->string map). Kept permissive.

maxUnavailable.description: Max pods a voluntary disruption may take at once (integer or a percentage string like '25%'). Never set so high that a drain can break majority quorum vs replicas.

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level security context (built for the PSS restricted profile).

runAsNonRoot.description: Require the container to run as a non-root user.

runAsUser.description: UID to run as (image runs as 65532).

fsGroup.description: Supplemental group owning the PVC so the nonroot user can write it.

fsGroupChangePolicy.description: When to chown the volume for fsGroup. OnRootMismatch avoids a slow recursive chown on every mount of a large data_dir.

containerSecurityContext.description: Container-level security context (built for the PSS restricted profile).

allowPrivilegeEscalation.description: Whether the process can gain more privileges than its parent.

readOnlyRootFilesystem.description: Mount the container root filesystem read-only.

terminationGracePeriodSeconds.description: Graceful termination window. Must exceed preStop.sleepSeconds + connection drain + save-on-exit or the kubelet SIGKILLs mid-save and truncates the snapshot (arithmetic relation not schema-enforceable). Raise to 120-180 for datasets over ~5GB or slow storage.

startupProbe.description: Startup probe: suspends liveness/readiness until load-on-boot (and, in Raft mode, leader election) completes.

failureThreshold.description: Failure threshold. Startup budget = failureThreshold * periodSeconds; size to the worst-case reload/election time.

preStop.description: preStop lame-duck sleep so the Service endpoint deprograms before drain. Uses the native SleepAction (auto-gated on cluster >= 1.29; omitted on older clusters).

sleepSeconds.description: Lame-duck sleep seconds. 0 disables. Keep comfortably under terminationGracePeriodSeconds.

partition.description: Canary lever: freeze ordinals < partition, verify, then patch to 0 to roll the rest. 0 = roll everything.

minReadySeconds.description: How long a pod must stay ready before the rollout advances to the next ordinal (the rollout soak lever; a flapping pod cannot march the rollout through the cluster).

topologySpread.description: Optional topologySpreadConstraints so quorum survives a ZONE outage. Enable on multi-AZ; leave off on single-zone / k3s edge.

maxSkew.description: Maximum permitted skew across topology domains.

topologyKey.description: Topology key to spread across (default the zone label).

whenUnsatisfiable.description: Behavior when the constraint cannot be satisfied. ScheduleAnyway keeps a constrained cluster schedulable (best-effort).

initImage.description: Init container image (BusyBox) that stamps the per-pod cluster_announce_id from the StatefulSet ordinal.

console.description: The IronCache monitoring/management console: a separate, stateless Deployment. Disabled by default.

replicas.description: Stateless console replicas. 2+ gives HA (and a PDB).

tag.description: Console image tag. Empty defaults to the chart appVersion (published in lockstep with the cache).

httpPort.description: Single HTTP port serving /livez + /readyz + /metrics + the UI + /api/*.

seeds.description: Free-form list of host:port seed strings the console polls (point at the cache client Service DNS). When console.enabled, set this or /readyz never goes ready (optional in the template, so not required).

prometheusUrl.description: Shared Prometheus URL for the HA-consistent history backend. Empty falls back to per-replica embedded history (each replica shows a different trend window).

nodeUser.description: Least-privilege ACL user the console dials nodes as.

nodePasswordSecret.description: Node-dial password source (mounted as a file; never inline).

existingSecret.description: Existing Secret name holding the node password.

tokensSecret.description: API RBAC tokens (read + admin). Configuring a token flips the console into enforce mode.

existingSecret.description: Existing Secret name holding the tokens.

resources.description: Console container resource requests/limits. Free-form quantity maps.

maxUnavailable.description: Max console pods unavailable during a voluntary disruption (integer or percentage string).

networkPolicy.description: Console NetworkPolicy (SSRF-containment, #369). Defaults ON (unlike the top-level networkPolicy). Needs a NetworkPolicy-aware CNI (inert / fails-open otherwise).

allowDNS.description: Allow DNS egress. Keep on: the console resolves the cache Service DNS and the history-backend host by name.

ingressFrom.description: Free-form NetworkPolicyPeer allowlist for the console HTTP port (empty = open on that port).

prometheusEgress.description: Free-form egress peer(s) matching console.prometheusUrl. Left empty, history queries are blocked (fail closed).

extraEgress.description: Free-form extra egress rules appended verbatim.
